pages.full_text:
looking East across the Picos after
crossing the pass at Ponton

bottle with a bear + honey on for lunch.
It turned out to be brandy. By the time Eve
had drunk Charles's + Thomas's + her own, she
was in a much better mood. We had lunch
by grassy meadowland + apple trees near a
salmon river.
	We drove on up River Sella, a gorgeous
gorge, with the peaks partly in cloud. We
crossed over at Ponton Pass, 1280 m. low on
petrol.
	In the north it was green + they grew
maize. There were cows near the pass. Once
over the pass the scenery is yellower with
Med Scrub.
	We stopped to have tea at Riano which is on
a mega lake, a small marina + modern houses
pages.route_sketch_description: Hand-drawn mountain profile/skyline sketch at the top of the page, showing the view looking East across the Picos after crossing the pass at Ponton. Shows a rough outline of mountain ridges with vertical lines suggesting cliffs or slopes on the left.
photo_captions: looking East across the Picos after crossing the pass at Ponton
pages.notes: The sketch caption reads 'looking East across the Picos after crossing the pass at Ponton' — this is a hand-drawn landscape/route sketch rather than a photo caption. The year is not stated on this page but context (Spain/Picos de Europa trip, Monday 31st July visible on right page) suggests summer; volume A covers 1989-2003. 'Med Scrub' likely means Mediterranean scrubland. 'Riano' could also be read as 'Riaño' (a reservoir town in León, Spain).

pages.full_text:
red tiled roof
hooks for onions
door to store maize
hanging [?EP]
wood beams
stilts with mushroom
baffles
steps

Anti-rat house. horreos.
north of Picos. For storing crops

	We drive on along a flat valley,
very hot with the sun in our eyes. Most
villages have a stork's nest on the church. * *
On to Mansillas Los Mulas + camp at __Esla__
small, pretty clean. They are building tennis courts.
	Monday 31st July	[?Irrigation from the lakes/dams make]
				[?the first part of the plain fertile.]
	We bought coffee at the Bar, on the
campsite, because we've run out of
coffee. We reached Leon, which we liked.
Until the beginning of this century, the pop.
was 30,000. Now it is [strikethrough: 100,] 130,000, so there is
a band of modern flats around the old town.
The cathedral dominates cf Beauvais, but many
flying buttresses, so it didn't fall down.
Grills around monuments - Charles's question to
me "Mummy, why don't they let you see
Jesus?"
3 storks' nests on cathedral.
pages.notes: Top of page contains a labelled sketch of an 'Anti-rat house' (horreo) with annotations: red tiled roof, hooks for onions, door to store maize, hanging [?EP - possibly 'EP' or another abbreviation unclear], wood beams, stilts with mushroom baffles, steps. The marginal annotation beside 'Monday 31st July' is partially obscured/cramped and reads approximately 'Irrigation from the lakes/dams make the first part of the plain fertile.' — transcribed as best guess. 'Mansillas Los Mulas' likely refers to 'Mansilla de las Mulas' (town in León province, Spain). 'Esla' is the River Esla in León. Year cannot be confirmed from page content alone — Monday 31st July falls in e.g. 1989, 1995, 2000 within the volume's range; cannot assert without further context. The '100' before 130,000 appears struck through in the original.
